This page shows how the embedded screenshots fit together as a working Notion setup for Seminole Sharks Swim & Dive Team: A season tracker for the Seminole Sharks' Madison All-City League swim & dive season, built around Team Unify registration, pay-by-check scholarship status, and t-shirt coordination.

What this is

A preview of a duplicate-ready Notion client tracker organized around the public workflow visible on the site.

Public workflow used

HEY SHARKS!!, Double check t shirt sizes when registering. There are youth and adult sizes. All kids get a t shirt with registration, If your child qualifies for the free membership this summer (13 and up only, final or only swimmer/diver on team), click pay by check and email gennazenner@gmail.com to let me know your child is in in this category, Team Unify calendar is updated with meet information

Season ops snapshot Registration + roster board Family record (Hollenbeck family) Registration + Volunteer view Season calendar + All-City meets Season closeout + re-enrollment Setup map

Why this shape

From overview to usable workspace

The first screenshots show the main views. The later screenshots show the editable structure that would be delivered: fields, focused tables, individual client pages, and starter records.

What it helps with

  • One board for every Sharks family's Team Unify registration, pay-by-check / free-membership status, and t-shirt size — no more switching between Gmail threads and Team Unify tabs.
  • Season calendar pulls the Team Unify meet schedule alongside the pool's own practice windows so parents stop asking 'when is the next meet?' in your inbox.
  • Scholarship / free-membership requests land in a queue with one-click 'confirmed via pay-by-check' status so Genna stops hand-matching email replies to Team Unify records.
